task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: Function works as expected, handles empty list correctly
trace: The function first sorts the input list and removes duplicates by converting it to a set. After that, we iterate through the sorted list and group consecutive numbers into ranges while avoiding creating ranges of single numbers. In case of an empty list, it returns an empty string. An edge case this function handles is when there are no duplicate or adjacent numbers in the input list (e.g., [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]).
